chunk_summary: This section discusses case-based decision pathways for managing pediatric metabolic risks, including MASLD, hypertension, genetic dyslipidemia, and obesity-related conditions, with specific diagnostic and treatment actions outlined for each case.
learning_objectives: Understand the diagnostic criteria and management strategies for MASLD in children, including the role of ALT and imaging.
Learn the evaluation and treatment approach for persistent stage 1 hypertension in pediatric patients.
Recognize the diagnostic thresholds and management steps for suspected familial hypercholesterolemia in children.
Identify the interplay of obesity, reproductive health, and mental health in metabolic risk management.
key_facts: ALT >2× sex-specific upper limit is clinically significant for MASLD, even with normal ultrasound findings.
Persistent stage 1 hypertension requires confirmation with ABPM and metabolic comorbidity screening.
LDL-C ≥190 mg/dL strongly suggests familial hypercholesterolemia, even with unclear family history.
Structured lifestyle therapy is a first-line treatment for MASLD and obesity-related hypertension.
Cascade screening of parents is recommended for children with suspected familial hypercholesterolemia.
Mental health conditions, such as binge eating, can act as metabolic modifiers in obesity management.
reference_quote: ALT >2× sex-specific upper limit is clinically significant regardless of imaging. (Reference 1: ISPGHAN/Indian Pediatrics, 2024)
document_key_topics: MASLD diagnosis and management in children
Obesity and its metabolic and mental health impacts
